channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Blind UNILAG student overcomes brain tumour to become top JAMB candidate
item.description: Ogunsua-Dixon Tijesuni, a blind student at the University of Lagos (UNILAG), has defied all odds by becoming the top scorer in the Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) exam. Her journey has...

item.title: U.S. Goalkeeper's World Cup Ends in Heartbreaking Loss
item.description: Matt Freese's World Cup journey for the U.S. men's national team ended in heartbreak after a critical error during the tournament. Entering the competition as a key question mark in the squad, Freese'...

item.title: Kenya Police Set Up Checkpoints to Control Saba Saba Protests
item.description: Police in Kenya have announced enhanced security measures, including checkpoints across Nairobi, ahead of planned Saba Saba protests. The National Police Service (NPS) stated that these checkpoints ai...

item.title: Heavy Rains Disrupt Mumbai and Surrounding Areas, Schools Closed for Safety
item.description: The Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation (BMC) has declared all government, private, and civic-run schools and colleges in Mumbai closed on Tuesday, July 7, 2026, due to an 'orange' alert for heavy rain...

item.title: Ombudsman Dismisses Mao Aplasca Over Misconduct and Duty Neglect
item.description: The Ombudsman has officially dismissed Mao Aplasca from his position due to allegations of grave misconduct and neglect of duty. This decision comes after a thorough investigation into his actions, wh...

item.title: Belgium Defeats USA in World Cup Round of 16, Sets Up Spain Quarter-Final Clash
item.description: Belgium delivered a crushing 4-1 defeat to the United States in their Round of 16 clash at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, ending the USA's hopes of reaching their first quarter-final in 24 years. The match,...

item.title: Age-related Fertility Challenges Persist Even With Donor Eggs
item.description: New research suggests that older women, even when using young donor eggs, face significant challenges in achieving pregnancy and live births. A study analyzing data from 1,774 women found that those a...

item.title: China Conducts Long-Range Missile Test in Pacific Ocean, Sparks U.S. Concern
item.description: China conducted a long-range missile test into the Pacific Ocean, prompting concern from U.S. officials. The test, described by Chinese naval spokesperson Wang Xuemeng as a routine military training e...

item.title: Man Arrested After Graffiti Stand-Off on Melbourne Bridge
item.description: A man has been arrested following a stand-off on Melbourne's Bolte Bridge after he spray-painted a graffiti image of 'Pam the Bird'. The incident, which occurred on July 7, 2026, involved a confrontat...

item.title: ACT-Wazalendo Calls for Respectful Political Discourse on Social Media
item.description: ACT-Wazalendo, a major opposition party in Tanzania, has criticized the rise of insult-driven politics on social media, urging for more constructive debates. The party warns that personal attacks and ...

item.title: Chennai's 50-year-old pottery legacy: Family-run business thrives despite challenges
item.description: K. Manohar, a 65-year-old resident of Royapettah, has been making small curd pots (dahi handi) for 50 years. His family-run enterprise, operated with his wife Bhuvaneshwari, faces modern challenges li...

item.title: Police Chief Feroz Khan Linked to Corruption Scandal Involving R50m and R280m Contracts
item.description: The Madlanga commission has revealed evidence that Police Crime Intelligence boss Maj-Gen Feroz Khan was involved in corrupt practices linked to two high-value tenders. Khan allegedly received kickbac...

item.title: Prince Harry Travels to UK Without Family Amid Security and Accommodation Dispute
item.description: Prince Harry has arrived in the United Kingdom without his wife, Meghan Markle, and their children, Archie and Lilibet, following unresolved tensions with Buckingham Palace over security arrangements ...

item.title: Windsor-Essex Faces Multi-Day Cleanup After Weekend Storms
item.description: The article details the ongoing cleanup efforts in Windsor-Essex following the devastating weekend storms that caused widespread damage. The cleanup is expected to take several days, with authorities ...

item.title: Summer Tides Revellers Sleep on Beach After Night Out, Spark Social Media Reactions
item.description: A video showing revellers sleeping on the beach after a night of partying during the Malindi event at Lost Beach Club has gone viral on social media. The clip captures young women sprawled on the sand...

item.title: Melbourne's four-day protest surge highlights growing social tensions
item.description: Melbourne has long been dubbed the protest capital of Australia, and recent events underscored this reputation with four demonstrations occurring within a single day. The protests spanned various caus...

item.title: Impeachment Trial of Vice President Sara Duterte Begins
item.description: The impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte formally commenced on July 6, 2026, with Senator Francis 'Chiz' Escudero elected as the presiding officer. The trial involves allegations under Art...

item.title: Local Woman Found Dead in Desert After Months-Long Search
item.description: A 55-year-old woman from Piñon Hills, California, was found dead in a desert area near her home on June 30 after being reported missing since May 25. San Bernardino County sheriff’s deputies responded...

item.title: Nigeria reduces import tariffs on used and new vehicles to boost trade
item.description: The Nigerian government, through the Customs Service, has reduced import tariffs on used vehicles from 15% to 5% and new vehicles from 20% to 10% as part of the 2026 fiscal policy. Comptroller-General...

item.title: 7-year-old boy dies after falling into river in Quezon City
item.description: A 7-year-old boy died after falling into a river in Quezon City, according to a news report. The incident occurred in Barangay Bahay Toro, where the boy was found unconscious by nearby residents. Emer...
